database_description: TODThis data is a modified version of the PKDD02 Discovery Challenge database which includes removing tests with null values. It contains data on the laboratory examinations of hepatitis B and C infected patients. The examinations were realized between 1982 and 2001 on 771 patients. The data are organized in 7 tables (4 entity tables, 3 relationship tables and 16 descriptive attributes). They contain basic information about the patients, results of biopsy, information on interferon therapy, results of out-hospital examinations, results of in-hospital examinations.

bio.description: Biopsy-related table containing histological findings for hepatitis cases, including fibrosis stage and inflammatory activity.

comments.fibros: Liver fibrosis stage stored as a discretized categorical value. It has 5 ordered levels with range 0-4, corresponding to fibrosis stages F0-F4.
comments.activity: Histological inflammatory activity grade stored as a discretized categorical value. In this standardized version it is represented as 5 ordered levels with range 0-4;
comments.b_id: Primary key of the Bio table, representing the biopsy record ID.

dispat.description: TODOCentral patient/case table containing the main entity for hepatitis classification, including patient identifier, demographic attributes, and the target label.

comments.m_id: Primary key of the dispat table and target ID of the dataset.
comments.sex: Sex of the patient, stored as a categorical varchar value.Usually: 1 = Male, 2 = Female
comments.age: Age of the patient, stored as a discretized categorical varchar value rather than a continuous number.Mapping relationships: 0: <20 years old, 1: 20-29 years old, 2: 30-39 years old, 3: 40-49 years old, 4: 50-59 years old, 5: 60-69 years old, 6: 70+ years old
comments.type: Target label of the dataset, representing the hepatitis type classification.0 = Hepatitis B (B), 1 = Hepatitis C (C)

indis.description: In-hospital examination table containing discretized laboratory test results for hepatitis cases.

comments.got: Serum GOT/AST (aspartate aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels.
comments.gpt: Serum GPT/ALT (alanine aminotransferase), stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate higher enzyme levels.
comments.alb: Albumin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher albumin level.
comments.tbil: Total bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher total bilirubin level.
comments.dbil: Direct bilirubin, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 2 levels with range 0-1, where larger values indicate a higher direct bilirubin level.
comments.che: Cholinesterase, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 10 levels with range 0-9, where larger values indicate a higher cholinesterase level.
comments.ttt: Thymol turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result.
comments.ztt: Zinc sulfate turbidity test, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 6 levels with range 0-5, where larger values indicate a higher test result.
comments.tcho: Total cholesterol, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher cholesterol level.
comments.tp: Total protein, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 4 levels with range 0-3, where larger values indicate a higher total protein level.
comments.in_id: Primary key of the indis table, representing the in-hospital examination record ID.

inf.description: Interferon-therapy table containing treatment-related information for hepatitis cases.

comments.dur: Duration of interferon therapy, stored as an ordered discretized categorical value. It has 5 levels with range 0-4; larger values indicate a longer treatment duration.
comments.a_id: Primary key of the inf table, representing the interferon-treatment record ID.

rel11.description: Relation table linking hepatitis cases in `dispat` to biopsy records in `bio`.

comments.b_id: Foreign key referencing `bio.b_id`, identifying the biopsy record linked to a hepatitis case.
comments.m_id: Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to a biopsy record.

rel12.description: Relation table linking hepatitis cases in `dispat` to in-hospital examination records in `indis`.

comments.in_id: Foreign key referencing `indis.in_id`, identifying the in-hospital examination record linked to a hepatitis case.
comments.m_id: Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to an examination record.

rel13.description: Relation table linking hepatitis cases in `dispat` to interferon-therapy records in `inf`.

comments.a_id: Foreign key referencing `inf.a_id`, identifying the interferon-therapy record linked to a hepatitis case.
comments.m_id: Foreign key referencing `dispat.m_id`, identifying the hepatitis case linked to a therapy record.
